items.caption: A graph titled "dividend stability," illustrating the relationship between the firm's dividend payments and a trend line over time. the graph is divided into two sections: the left side shows the "rs per share," which is a measure of the dividend paid out as a percentage of earnings, and the right side shows the "dividends per share," which is the actual amount of dividends paid out. the trend line represents the stability of the firm's dividend payments over time, indicating that the firm maintains a consistent position relative to the trend line. the graph is set against a light background with a grid for precise measurement.
